Protein names: tRNA (guanosine(18)-2'-O)-methyltransferase TARBP1 (EC 2.1.1.34) (TAR RNA-binding protein 1) (TAR RNA-binding protein of 185 kDa) (TRP-185)
Protein families: Class IV-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, RNA methyltransferase TrmH family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class IV-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. RNA methyltransferase TrmH family. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=guanosine(18) in tRNA + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 2'-O-methylguanosine(18) in tRNA + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:20077, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:10190, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:10192, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:74269, ChEBI:CHEBI:74445; EC=2.1.1.34; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:38867004}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:20078; Evidence={ECO:0000305|PubMed:38867004};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: S-adenosyl-L-methionine-dependent 2'-O-ribose methyltransferase that catalyzes the formation of 2'-O-methylguanosine at position 18 (Gm18) in a subset of tRNA (PubMed:18412263, PubMed:31019095, PubMed:38867004). Selectively mediates Gm18 methylation of tRNAGln-TTG/CTG and tRNASer-TGA/GCT. Gm18 modification can enhance the stability of modified tRNAs (PubMed:38867004). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:18412263, ECO:0000269|PubMed:31019095, ECO:0000269|PubMed:38867004}.; FUNCTION: (Microbial infection) In case of infection by HIV-1, it binds to the loop region of TAR RNA, a region also bound by RNA polymerase II (PubMed:7638159, PubMed:8626763, PubMed:8846792). Binding of TARBP1 and RNA polymerase II to HIV-1 TAR RNA is mutually exclusive, suggesting that TARBP1 may function alone or in conjunction with HIV-1 Tat to disengage RNA polymerase II from HIV-1 TAR RNA (PubMed:7638159, PubMed:8626763, PubMed:8846792). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:7638159, ECO:0000269|PubMed:8626763, ECO:0000269|PubMed:8846792}.

Protein families: Class IV-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, RNA methyltransferase TrmH family
Domain [CC]: DOMAIN: Both N- and C-terminal regions function in tRNA binding, but the substrate tRNA is determined by the catalytic domain. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:23867454}.
